content.observe: The kernel needs its own memory too, in small, oddly-sized chunks, allocated far more often than user processes ever request memory.
content.fault: A tiny kernel object just consumed an entire allocation unit. Without slab-style allocation, size mismatches like this waste real memory.

log: [kmem] request: 64 bytes for inode cache object
[kmem] slab cache 'inode_cache' has free object, allocating
[kmem] no slab fits, falling back to buddy allocator
[kmem] buddy allocator: splitting 8KB block into two 4KB blocks
